Feature.Description: Ardea labware handling: pick (and later put) a labware by orchestrating the DENSO robot (b-CAP tasks) and the KEYENCE hand (KV COM+). PickLabware runs the approach task, closes the hand, runs the retract task, and confirms the robot returned to the retract pose. Only allowed when the robot starts at a movable pose (base or retract). The robot-operating signal locks the carriage out during robot motion and is always cleared afterwards.

Command.Description: Pick a labware: verify the robot is at a movable pose, run the pick-approach task, close the hand, run the pick-retract task (which returns the robot to the retract pose), and confirm the retract pose. Intermediate responses report the current phase. The robot-operating signal is set during robot motion and always cleared when the command finishes.

Response.Description: True if the robot ended at the retract pose after the pick.

IntermediateResponse.Description: The current phase of the pick sequence.

Command.Description: Place a labware: verify the robot is at the retract pose (not the base pose) and the carriage is at the origin, run the approach task, open the hand, run the retract task (which returns the robot to the retract pose), and confirm the retract pose. Intermediate responses report the current phase.

Response.Description: True if the robot ended at the retract pose after the put.

IntermediateResponse.Description: The current phase of the put sequence.

DefinedExecutionError.Description: The robot is not at the retract pose, which PutLabware requires (the base pose is not allowed).
The robot is at neither the base pose nor the retract pose, so the pick cannot start.
The travel carriage is not at 0 mm; PickLabware requires the carriage at the origin.
The hand is not fully open; PickLabware requires the hand open before starting.
The robot did not return to the retract pose after the pick-retract task.
Failed to connect to or communicate with the robot controller over b-CAP.
Failed to acquire the robot or read the current joint angles.
A DENSO task (PAC program) did not start or finish within the configured timeout.
A DENSO task failed to run or stopped abnormally.
Failed to connect to or communicate with the PLC over KV COM+.
Failed to read or write a PLC device.
The hand failed to activate or to complete a chuck/unchuck within the timeout.
